description: A platform/party-master record for a person who can authenticate and act in the system. Pre-seeded (sign-up disabled); authenticates by email OTP or SSO. Carries the four RBAC grant sources' anchors (role, designation, businessUnit, plus direct user grants) and the per-approver auto-approval limit. Active status is re-checked every request, forcing logout on deactivation. The user is the actor every record's owning role, assignee, approver, requester, maker, and checker resolves to.

fields.name: userId
fields.label: User No.
fields.purpose: Stable internal key every record that names a person (requesterId, assigneeUserIds, approverUserIds, makerId, checkerId) foreign-keys to; the identity the audit log records as actorId.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email
fields.validation: trimmed; lowercased; unique case-insensitive; valid email format; the OTP/SSO sign-in identity; pre-validated on send and sign-in
fields.purpose: The login identity the email-OTP/SSO auth keys on and the address notifications send to; unique lowercased so the same person cannot be seeded twice and case never splits an account.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.validation: non-blank; the display field for every user reference (requester/approver/assignee normalizer -> name)
fields.purpose: The human-readable name shown wherever a user is referenced (approver dropdowns, requisition requester, audit narrative); the display field the entity normalizer resolves user ids to.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: default ACTIVE; INACTIVE re-checked every request and forces logout on the next call; INACTIVE users are not eligible assignees/approvers
fields.purpose: Gates whether the account can authenticate and be selected as an assignee/approver; the per-request re-check on INACTIVE forces immediate logout so a deactivated account cannot continue a session.

fields.name: departmentId
fields.label: Department
fields.validation: an existing business unit; defaults a requisition's department and scopes department-level visibility
fields.purpose: The user's department; defaults the requisition department (which drives routing-rule selection) and scopes department-level visibility and spend analytics.

fields.name: designationId
fields.label: Designation
fields.validation: one of the 7 designation ranks; contributes designation-source permissions and the rank used in assignment eligibility
fields.purpose: The user's seniority; supplies one of the four permission grant sources and the rank the 'same-or-higher rank' assignment/reassignment eligibility check reads (axiom A5).

fields.name: businessUnitId
fields.label: Business Unit / Vertical
fields.validation: an existing business unit/vertical; contributes vertical-source permissions
fields.purpose: The user's vertical; supplies one of the four permission grant sources (the union of role + user-grant + designation + business-unit) and scopes vertical-level analytics.

fields.name: roleId
fields.label: Role
fields.validation: an existing role (SystemAdmin/User/...); the primary permission grant source
fields.purpose: The user's role; the primary of the four permission grant sources whose union forms the effective permission set, and what the SystemAdmin (all) short-circuit keys on.

fields.name: userPermissions
fields.label: Direct Permissions
fields.validation: namespaced permission values granted directly to this user; unioned with role/designation/vertical permissions
fields.purpose: Per-user permission overrides (the UserRole grant source) so an individual can hold a permission their role lacks; one of the four sources deduplicated into the effective set, applied next request (no caching).

fields.name: approvalLimit
fields.label: Approval Limit
fields.validation: >= 0; base currency; null reads as the tenant default 200000; a configured 0 stays 0 and approves nothing; never falls back from an explicit 0
fields.purpose: The auto-approval ceiling the engine compares amountInBase against (axiom A7) and the nearest-bucket selection uses to reserve high-limit approvers for large amounts; not budget control (axiom A4). A 0 forces manual review for any positive amount.

invariants: A5 Finance/Management stages are approval-only; assignment requires assigner designation rank >= the stage's required rank
A6 SoD: a user never approves their own requisition/PO; maker != checker; buyer != PO approver; receiver != invoice approver
A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
effective permissions = deduplicated union of role + direct user grant + designation + business-unit, plus super-admin all; applied next request (no caching)
email unique case-insensitive lowercased; INACTIVE forces logout on next request
